name: Hubitat Elevation Local API
what_it_does: Local REST API for Hubitat Elevation smart home hub — controlling devices (lights, locks, thermostats, sensors), running rules, and accessing automation state entirely on local network without cloud dependency or latency.
use_cases: Controlling smart home devices locally without cloud latency or internet dependency
Reading sensor state (motion, temperature, humidity) for local automation agents
Triggering Hubitat rules and automations programmatically from external systems
Building local home dashboards with real-time device state polling
Integrating Hubitat with other local services like Home Assistant or Node-RED
not_for: Remote access without VPN or Hubitat Cloud — local API is LAN-only by default
Cloud-based smart home platforms (use Home Assistant cloud, SmartThings API)
Large commercial building automation (Hubitat targets residential use)
best_when: An agent runs locally on the home network and needs low-latency, privacy-first smart home control without cloud dependency.
avoid_when: You need remote access from outside the home network without a VPN, or need a large ecosystem of cloud-connected services.

auth.notes: Access token generated in Hubitat Admin UI. Passed as query parameter or header. No OAuth — local API only. Token scoped to specific apps/devices via Maker API app configuration. Single token per Maker API instance.

pricing.paid_tiers: Hubitat C-8 hub: ~$130 one-time; API access included
pricing.notes: No recurring subscription fees. API access built into the hardware hub. Optional cloud dashboard subscription ($30/year) for remote access features. Local control is free forever with hardware purchase.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits; local network bandwidth is the constraint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Community documentation more comprehensive than official docs. REST API simple and consistent. Maker API app configuration determines which devices are exposed. WebSocket event streaming available for real-time device updates.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device state reads are idempotent. Device commands (e.g., toggle) are not idempotent. Direct state commands (setLevel, on, off) are safer for agents than toggle commands.
known_agent_gotchas: Local-only by default — agents must be on LAN or use VPN for remote control
Hub IP may change without static IP/DHCP reservation — use hostname
Maker API app must explicitly expose devices — not all devices available by default
Hub reboots during firmware updates — agents need reconnection handling

security_score_components.security_notes: Local-only API means reduced attack surface. TLS optional on local network (HTTP often used locally). Access token in query parameter can appear in logs. No cloud exposure by default — privacy-first architecture. Token scoping via Maker API app.
